title: rosa woodsii var. granulifera
intro: Rosa woodsii var. granulifera, also known as the Rocky Mountain rose, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Rosa woodsii species. It is a shrub native to the western United States and Canada, known for its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Rosa woodsii var. granulifera include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties, and it can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture through various herbal preparations.
